target: Internal GEMMs matrix_mul / matrix_mul_complex used by eval_eig (Eig primitive, real eigendecomposition via QR iteration)

lever.description: Reorder matrix_mul and matrix_mul_complex from i-j-k to i-k-j loop order. The original inner k-loop read b[k*n+j] (stride n down a column of B = a cache miss per multiply); the i-k-j inner j-loop streams a contiguous row of B and a contiguous row of C. Each output element still accumulates over k in ascending order (real and imaginary parts independently for the complex variant), so the result is bit-for-bit identical.

benchmarks.method: rch Criterion, eval_primitive_multi(Eig, 48x48 f64); baseline = i-j-k matmuls, optimized = i-k-j

after.tool: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'linalg/eig_48x48_f64'

benchmarks.note: eval_eig runs ~100 QR iterations, each with three O(n^3) operations: qr_decomposition (Gram-Schmidt) and the two matmuls optimized here. Reordering only the two matmuls yields 1.38x on the full primitive; the Gram-Schmidt step (not touched) is the remaining cost. Non-overlapping CIs.

score.note: Realized 1.38x on a real production LAPACK primitive (Eig), bit-exact. Below 2x speedup because the optimized matmuls are ~2/3 of each iteration's matmul-class work and Gram-Schmidt QR is untouched.

isomorphism_proof.floating_point: Per output element the accumulation order over k is unchanged (ascending k = 0..n-1); the complex variant accumulates re and im independently in the same order. IEEE-754 sums are bit-for-bit identical; no FMA fusion introduced.
isomorphism_proof.ordering: Only loop-nest visitation order changed; output positions/values identical.
isomorphism_proof.rng: No RNG state is read or written.
isomorphism_proof.bit_identity_proof: New test matrix_mul_ikj_bit_identical_to_ijk multiplies 11x11 real and complex matrices and asserts f64::to_bits() equality (real + imaginary) vs inline i-j-k references. Existing eig/eigh/svd/qr unit + oracle conformance tests and the full fj-lax suite (1006/1006) pass. No synthetic golden-bits file: the to_bits parity test pins the result to the prior accumulation order.
